Mound |Other name = Hirata Iwaya Kofun | part_of = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District]][[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 28|coordinates = 6.45 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 48|coordinates = 12.32 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = 35m per side |burial Chamber = Bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |Associated Tombs = |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Buried Person = (Speculated) {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum) |designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_dot_label = Kanazuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Education|ref={{Harvid|Saiko|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kosaku Okabayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kanazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kanazuka Kofun (Revised Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Reading == * Reports ''' The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Mound |Other name = Hirata Iwaya Kofun | part_of = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District]][[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 28|coordinates = 6.45 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 48|coordinates = 12.32 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = 35m per side |burial Chamber = Bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |Associated Tombs = |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Buried Person = (Speculated) {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum) |designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_dot_label = Kanazuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shinji Saiko|year=2000|chapter=Study of the History of Asuka Region (1): notes on the Kanazuka Kofun, Associated Tomb of Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum|title={{PDFlink|[https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/kiyo/imgs/01.pdf Asuka Village designation1 Research Bulletin, Vol. 1]}}|publisher=Asuka Village Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Saiko|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kosaku Okabayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kanazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kanazuka Kofun (Revised Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Reading == * Reports ''' It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. Mound |Other name = Hirata Iwaya Kofun | part_of = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District]][[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 28|coordinates = 6.45 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 48|coordinates = 12.32 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 35m per side |burial Chamber = Bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |Associated Tombs = |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Buried Person = (Speculated) {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum) |designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Kanazuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Mausoleum|title={{PDFlink|[https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/kiyo/imgs/01.pdf Asuka Village designation1 Research Bulletin, Vol. 1]}}|publisher=Asuka Village Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Saiko|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kosaku Okabayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kanazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kanazuka Kofun (Revised Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Reading == * Reports ''' Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Education|ref={{Harvid|Saiko|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kosaku Okabayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kanazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|続 Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kanazuka Kofun (Revised Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Reading == * Reports ''' But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kanazuka Kofun (カナヅカ古墳) |image = File:Kanazuka Kofun (Asuka), funkyu.jpg |Other name = Hirata Iwaya Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.468472222222225|N|135.80341666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 35m per side |burial Chamber = Bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |Associated Tombs = |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Buried Person = (Speculated) {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum) |designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Kanazuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Education|ref={{Harvid|Saiko|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kosaku Okabayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kanazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Revised Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kanazuka Kofun (Revised Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Reading == * Reports ''' {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Kanazuka Kofun Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Kanazuka Kofun''' (カナヅカ古墳), also known as Hirata Iwaya Kofun, is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Hirata, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is square-shaped and Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]'s {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the Kanazuka Kofun was constructed on a flattened area cut into the southern slope of a hill between the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kinmei’s tomb) and {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}. Along the same hill slope, four kofun align east to west: the Umemiyama Kofun, Kanazuka Kofun, Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Sekishoin, and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja|野口王墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Oukou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the stone chamber of Kanazuka Kofun was already exposed. However, the chamber suffered significant destruction in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], and from 1995 onward, surveys were conducted to confirm the extent of the site. The kofun is square-shaped, measuring about 35 meters per side, and consists of two tiers. A large terrace measuring approximately 60 meters east-west and 25 meters north-south was constructed in front of the mound. A surrounding moat measuring 11.7 meters in width was identified on the northern side. The burial chamber is a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an opening to the south. The chamber has a total length of approximately 16 meters, and its stones are made of massive cut pieces of quartz diorite. The Kanazuka Kofun is estimated to date to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], during the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is uncertain, the close proximity to the Umemiyama Kofun has led to speculation that it is the actual burial site of {{ill|Princess Kibihime|ja|吉備姫王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kibihime|qq=}}, a gran11aughter of Emperor Kinmei who died in 643. == History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] ''' Early [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]], Hideane Kawauchi mentioned the Kanazuka as an associated tomb in the ''Shugishūkai''. ''' 1829 (Bunsei 12): Nagamichi Tsukawa described the exposed stone chamber as "Kanazuka" in the ''Ubana Diary''. ''' 1848 (Kaei 1): Sadamasa Kitanoura suggested in ''Uchiuminaru'' that the site is the tomb of Princess Kibihime. ''' 1854 (Kaei 7): Kiyokage Tsukui described the open stone chamber as "Iwaya" in the ''Seiseki Zushi''. ''' 1857 (Ansei 4): Yoshitomi Tanimori speculated it was Princess Kibihime's tomb in the ''Yamato Province Mausoleum Tour Diary''. ''' 1867 (Keiō 3): The site appeared in an Mausoleum map map_dot_labeled "Kanazuka, Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum". * 1890 (Meiji 23): Beikichi Miyake witnessed destruction of the stone chamber, as noted by Sadakichi Kida. * 1892 (Meiji 25): The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 the Kanazuka Kofun as an associated tomb of the {{ill|Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umeyama Kofun|lt=Umemiyama Kofun|ja|梅山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umemiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Hinokuma Sakai Mausoleum). * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Site boundary surveys were conducted by the Asuka Village Board of Education, with preliminary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 1999 (Heisei 11): Records from the Meiji period documenting the destruction of the chamber were discovered. == burial == The burial facility consists of a bilateral {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: approx. 16 meters * Main chamber: approx. 5.45 meters long, 3.64 meters wide, 2.7 meters high * Passageway: approx. 10 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.8 meters high The chamber features massive stones of cut quartz diorite. The main chamber walls are constructed in two tiers (three stones for the rear wall and four for each side wall), while the passageway walls are composed of one or two tiers (10 stones for each side). The ceiling consists of two stones in the main chamber and three in the passageway. The chamber design is similar to the "Iwayayama-style" stone chamber found in the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] (also in Asuka), though it is slightly later and precedes the construction of {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite
